sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Matthew Rogers, is a 14-year-old male who presents for an acne follow-up.
He reports that his acne has been improving, stating that it is "definitely" better.

He reports using the prescribed medications as instructed, including clindamycin lotion, tretinoin cream, 4-5% benzoyl peroxide wash for the face, and 5-10% benzoyl peroxide wash for the chest and back.
He denies any redness, peeling, dryness, or irritation from the medications.
He has not been using the 5-10% benzoyl peroxide wash on the chest and back as directed, stating that it hasn't bothered him.
He expresses interest in addressing the acne on his chest and back, but is not currently concerned about it.
Past medical history: NOT MENTIONED
Review of systems: NOT MENTIONED
Current medications: Clindamycin lotion, tretinoin cream, 4-5% benzoyl peroxide wash for the face, 5-10% benzoyl peroxide wash for the chest and back (not currently using).
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has scattered inflammatory papules and pustules across the upper back and posterior shoulders, with less than 5 inflammatory papules on the forehead, cheeks, and chin.
Acne vulgaris is improved and well-controlled on the face, but there is a mild flare on the back.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has acne vulgaris, with improved and well-controlled lesions on the face, but a mild flare on the back.
Problem list: Acne vulgaris; Acne vulgaris (status: active)
Plan:
I recommended continuing the current treatment plan for the face, which includes clindamycin lotion, tretinoin cream, and 4-5% benzoyl peroxide wash.
I initiated treatment with PanOxyl 10% foaming wash for the back in the shower daily.
I sent home with samples and coupons for the PanOxyl 10% foaming wash.
I also sent in a new refill for clindamycin with 11 refills and tretinoin cream with 3 refills.
Follow-up: I plan to see the patient back in one year for a follow-up appointment.
